commands.repair_reason: - [P1] Remaining action is maintainer API/timing acceptance plus contributor proof, not a narrow automation-safe repair.; Cleared: No concrete security or supply-chain regression was found; the diff does not touch dependencies, workflows, secrets, or command execution, and the new hook payloads do not embed transcript text. (sha=32a0e90de50369b363e7caed6e420710235dce54)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds documented ACP `agent:turn:end` and `agent:turn:save` internal hook events, save-outcome callback wiring for manager/direct/dispatch ACP turns, and focused tests/docs.

PR surface: Source +338, Tests +952, Docs +9. Total +1299 across 17 files.

Reproducibility: not applicable. this is a feature PR rather than a bug report. Source checks show current main and `v2026.5.28` do not have the requested hook surface, while the PR tests exercise the proposed paths.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Remaining action is maintainer API/timing acceptance plus contributor proof, not a narrow automation-safe repair.

target.title: feat(hooks): add agent:turn:end and agent:turn:save internal hook events
target.body:
## Summary

- Adds two ACP turn internal hook events:
  - `agent:turn:end` for ACP runtime turn completion or failure.
  - `agent:turn:save` for the save-layer outcome after transcript persistence is saved, skipped, or fails.
- Wires dispatch-backed ACP turns and direct ACP command turns through the awaited save callback before reporting the save outcome.
- Adds `context.saveOutcome` (`saved`, `skipped`, `failed`) and optional `context.saveSkipReason`; `context.success` is true only when `saveOutcome === "saved"`.
- Requires explicit save evidence before reporting `saved`; missing callbacks and no-op callbacks report skipped outcomes instead.
- Validates structured save callback results and reports malformed results as failed save outcomes.
- Tracks actual transcript append evidence so blocked/no-op writes report `skipped` instead of `saved`.
- Documents the split contract and covers manager, dispatch, direct command, persistence, and type-guard paths with focused tests.

## Change Type

- [ ] Bug fix
- [x] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [x] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [x] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #37833
- Related #60734

## Behavior Changes

Internal hook consumers can subscribe to two ACP turn boundaries:

| Event | Fires when | Durable turn data readable |
|---|---|---|
| `agent:turn:end` | ACP runtime turn completes or fails after terminal event delivery | No |
| `agent:turn:save` | ACP save phase reaches an outcome | Yes, when `context.saveOutcome === "saved"` |

`agent:turn:end` carries `sessionKey`, `success`, `durationMs`, and optional `errorCode`.

`agent:turn:save` carries `sessionKey`, `success`, `saveOutcome`, `turnSuccess`, `durationMs`, optional `turnErrorCode`, optional `saveError`, and optional `saveSkipReason`.

For direct ACP and dispatch-backed ACP turns, transcript persistence now runs inside the awaited save callback before the save outcome is emitted. This is an internal lifecycle ordering change; there are no user-facing messaging or config behavior changes.

## Security Impact

- New permissions/capabilities? `No`
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? `No`
- New/changed network calls? `No`
- Command/tool execution surface changed? `No`
- Data access scope changed? `No`

## Verification

Commands run on head `32a0e90de50369b363e7caed6e420710235dce54`:

```text
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/acp/control-plane/manager.test.ts src/hooks/internal-hooks.test.ts src/agents/command/attempt-execution.cli.test.ts src/commands/agent.acp.test.ts src/commands/agent.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts src/agents/agent-command.live-model-switch.test.ts
pnpm check:test-types
pnpm exec oxlint src/acp/control-plane/manager.core.ts src/acp/control-plane/manager.types.ts src/acp/control-plane/manager.test.ts src/acp/control-plane/manager.turn-runner.ts src/hooks/internal-hooks.ts src/hooks/internal-hooks.test.ts src/agents/agent-command.ts src/agents/command/attempt-execution.ts src/agents/command/attempt-execution.cli.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp.ts src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp-transcript.runtime.ts src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts src/commands/agent.acp.test.ts src/commands/agent.test.ts src/agents/agent-command.live-model-switch.test.ts
pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 docs/automation/hooks.md src/acp/control-plane/manager.core.ts src/acp/control-plane/manager.types.ts src/acp/control-plane/manager.test.ts src/acp/control-plane/manager.turn-runner.ts src/hooks/internal-hooks.ts src/hooks/internal-hooks.test.ts src/agents/agent-command.ts src/agents/command/attempt-execution.ts src/agents/command/attempt-execution.cli.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp.ts src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp-transcript.runtime.ts src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts src/commands/agent.acp.test.ts src/commands/agent.test.ts src/agents/agent-command.live-model-switch.test.ts
git diff --check origin/main...HEAD
git diff --check
```

Result:

```text
5 Vitest shards passed: manager, hooks, ACP transcript persistence, direct ACP command, dispatch ACP, dispatch-from-config, ACP live-model-switch command
Test type check passed
Touched-file oxlint passed
Touched-file formatting check passed
git diff --check passed
```

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: ACP save readiness is now reported only after the awaited save callback reaches a validated outcome. Direct ACP and dispatch ACP transcript persistence run inside that callback, so `saveOutcome: "saved"` means the application-level transcript append path completed and returned durable write evidence; `skipped` and `failed` are reported separately. Missing save callbacks and no-op save callbacks report skipped outcomes.
- **Real environment tested**: Local OpenClaw checkout on PR head `32a0e90de50369b363e7caed6e420710235dce54`, macOS, Node `v25.6.0`, pnpm `11.2.2`.
- **Exact steps or command run after the patch**: Ran a local `pnpm exec tsx --eval` command importing production `persistAcpTurnTranscript`, writing a prompt/reply pair through the ACP transcript persistence helper into a temporary session store, then reading the resulting transcript file.
- **Evidence after fix**: Redacted terminal capture from that command. The local command also printed existing config-validation warnings before the JSON, but exited successfully.

```json
{
  "saveOutcome": "saved",
  "saveSkipReason": null,
  "hasSessionFile": true,
  "transcriptHasPrompt": true,
  "transcriptHasReply": true,
  "transcriptLineCount": 3
}
```

- **Observed result after fix**: The ACP transcript persistence helper returned `saveOutcome: "saved"` only after producing a session file whose transcript contained both the prompt and reply text. Separate focused tests cover `no_save_callback`, `no_save_evidence`, malformed callback results, blocked message writes, dispatch ACP, and direct ACP command callback wiring.
- **What was not tested**: A networked external `acpx` adapter process.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? `Yes`
- Config/env changes? `No`
- Migration needed? `No`

## Failure Recovery

Revert this PR to remove the new internal hook events and return ACP turn completion to stats-only behavior.

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: Consumers may confuse runtime completion with durable transcript readiness.
- Mitigation: Separate `agent:turn:end` from `agent:turn:save`, with `saveOutcome` identifying `saved`, `skipped`, or `failed`.
- Risk: Slow transcript persistence could delay ACP cleanup.
- Mitigation: Only the save callback is awaited for the save boundary; hook handlers still use bounded fire-and-forget execution.
target.author: mantisai-bot

Direct ACP and dispatch ACP transcript persistence run inside that callback, so `saveOutcome: "saved"` means the application-level transcript append path completed and returned durable write evidence; `skipped` and `failed` are reported separately. Missing save callbacks and no-op save callbacks report skipped outcomes.
- **Real environment tested**: Local OpenClaw checkout on PR head `32a0e90de50369b363e7caed6e420710235dce54`, macOS, Node `v25.6.0`, pnpm `11.2.2`.
- **Exact steps or command run after the patch**: Ran a local `pnpm exec tsx --eval` command importing production `persistAcpTurnTranscript`, writing a prompt/reply pair through the ACP transcript persistence helper into a temporary session store, then reading the resulting transcript file.
- **Evidence after fix**: Redacted terminal capture from that command. The local command also printed existing config-validation warnings before the JSON, but exited successfully.

```json
{
  "saveOutcome": "saved",
  "saveSkipReason": null,
  "hasSessionFile": true,
  "transcriptHasPrompt": true,
  "transcriptHasReply": true,
  "transcriptLineCount": 3
}
```

- **Observed result after fix**: The ACP transcript persistence helper returned `saveOutcome: "saved"` only after producing a session file whose transcript contained both the prompt and reply text. Separate focused tests cover `no_save_callback`, `no_save_evidence`, malformed callback results, blocked message writes, dispatch ACP, and direct ACP command callback wiring.
- **What was not tested**: A networked external `acpx` adapter process.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? `Yes`
- Config/env changes? `No`
- Migration needed? `No`

## Failure Recovery

Revert this PR to remove the new internal hook events and return ACP turn completion to stats-only behavior.

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: Consumers may confuse runtime completion with durable transcript readiness.
- Mitigation: Separate `agent:turn:end` from `agent:turn:save`, with `saveOutcome` identifying `saved`, `skipped`, or `failed`.
- Risk: Slow transcript persistence could delay ACP cleanup.
- Mitigation: Only the save callback is awaited for the save boundary; hook handlers still use bounded fire-and-forget execution.
